name: Coinstar Kiosk
description_short: Coinstar kiosks convert loose coins into cash, eGift cards, or cryptocurrency at grocery stores nationwide — no sorting or rolling required.
description_long:
Coinstar operates a nationwide network of self-service kiosks found inside grocery stores across the United States. The company has undergone recent ownership changes, with Arctic Slope Regional Corporation (ASRC) completing its acquisition in February 2026. Coinstar also expanded its partnership with U.S. Bank in December 2025, broadening its financial institution transfer capabilities. Individual kiosks vary in services offered depending on location.

The core service is Coins to Cash, which lets users dump loose change into the kiosk and receive cash on the spot — no counting, sorting, or rolling required. Beyond coins, kiosks offer eGift card exchanges (with NO FEE options from select retailers), charity donations through the Coinstar Coins that Count program, and Amazon Cash loading (add $5–$500 directly to an Amazon account balance). Financial services include Transfer to Account (move coins or cash into a checking account), the CINQ by Coinstar debit card, and two crypto purchase pathways: CINQ by Coinstar (powered by Zero Hash, 25+ cryptocurrencies) and Coinme (cash-to-crypto via a separate app).

What sets Coinstar apart is its grocery store placement — the kiosks are accessible during routine shopping trips without requiring a separate bank visit. The no-fee eGift card option is a genuine value-add for consumers who would otherwise pay the standard coin-counting fee. The kiosks also serve as one of the more accessible on-ramps to cryptocurrency for unbanked or underbanked consumers who prefer to use physical cash.

The honest caveat is that the standard Coins to Cash fee is not prominently disclosed on the website and varies by location, which has historically drawn consumer complaints. Both crypto pathways (CINQ and Coinme) require downloading a mobile app before visiting the kiosk — a friction point for less tech-savvy users. Some kiosks, like the PayOMatic location profiled here, do not accept bills at all, limiting the cash services available. Coinstar is a convenient utility for occasional coin clearing, not a full financial services provider.
pros: No counting, sorting, or rolling required — just pour coins in and receive cash instantly
Located inside grocery stores for convenient access during routine shopping
eGift card option available with NO FEE from select retailers
Supports 25+ cryptocurrencies via CINQ by Coinstar (powered by Zero Hash)
Amazon Cash loading ($5–$500) with no hidden fees and immediate account credit
Charity donation option via Coinstar Coins that Count program
Bank account transfer available — bypasses bank branch visit for depositing coins or cash
cons: Coin-to-cash fee varies by location and is not transparently listed upfront on the website
Both crypto services (CINQ and Coinme) require downloading a separate mobile app before visiting
Some kiosks (including this PayOMatic location) do not accept bills, limiting cash service options
Not a banking substitute — no account opening, loans, or credit products
eGift card fees vary by retailer and brand; no-fee options are limited to select merchants
best_for: Consumers with large jars of loose change who want instant cash without bank coin counters
Unbanked or underbanked individuals looking for an accessible cash-to-crypto on-ramp
Shoppers who want to redirect coin value to Amazon purchases without a card
Anyone wanting to donate spare change to charity without managing envelopes or checks
diagnosis: Coinstar kiosks are best for consumers who accumulate loose change and want a fast, no-fuss conversion to cash, gift cards, or even crypto during a grocery run. The core coin-counting service charges a fee that varies by location, so consumers who are fee-sensitive should opt for the no-fee eGift card route or verify their local rate before using the cash option.
services: Coins to Cash (instant cash for loose coins, fees vary by location)
eGift Card exchange (NO FEE options available from select retailers)
Coinstar Coins that Count charity donation program
CINQ by Coinstar — cash or coins converted to 25+ cryptocurrencies (Zero Hash)
Coinme — cash-to-crypto purchase at crypto-enabled kiosks (app required)
Amazon Cash — load $5–$500 cash to Amazon account balance, no fees
Transfer to Account — deposit coins or cash directly into a checking account
